title: quercus × filialis
intro: Quercus × filialis, a hybrid of the pedunculate oak (Quercus robur) and the sessile oak (Quercus petraea), is a deciduous tree native to Europe, known for its large leaves and sturdy branches. The bark of Quercus × filialis has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its tannin content. Research has shown that the plant's extracts may have antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties, providing potential health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function. Quercus × filialis is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be administered to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
